title: alnus lanata
intro: Alnus lanata, also known as the white alder or mountain alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections due to its high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Alnus lanata include its potential to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Alnus lanata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
